publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 198-199. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. On my return from Esher, at eleven this morning, I was vexed at finding a letter from you of which only the envelope belonged to me; the contents being for my Mother. I conclude that you have sent to her one for me. I did not of course read the one I found; but I sent it to
The Paris affair has turned out most miserably & vexatiously. I cannot bear to think of it, & of the folly of so many people.--

I am endeavouring to do all you would most wise, dearest W. My scientific & literary pursuits must ever be the main thing evidently, for myself; & I quite make everything yield now (excepting considerations of health & exercise) to these
Don't say, & don't think, that my resolutions cannot be trusted to.--
However even if you do, there is the comfort of remembering that you are not God, Providence, or Fate; & that your fears & doubts cannot make me fail, if I do not fail of my own accord.
Still I should like you to be my good Fate, & to anticipate perseverance & success. Adieu.
N.B. Mr De Morgan has been with me all this morning.
